<parameter=code>

        print(f"  color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Let me look at the exact cells to the right of the blocks
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nDetailed view (rows 40-44, cols 22-30):")
for i in range(40, 45):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][22:30]}")
</parameter>
</function>

sections.content:
OK so the blocks are at cols 19-23, and the passage (c) extends to the right from col 24 onwards. The blocks can't move right even though there's clear passage. This is strange.
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the blocks can only move within a constrained area defined by the gray walls. Looking at the maze:
- The blocks are in a chamber that's bounded by gray walls
- The passage (c) might be a different area where blocks can't move freely
Actually, let me think about this differently. The blocks moved UP from rows 45-49 to rows 40-44. But when I tried to move RIGHT, they didn't move. Maybe the blocks can only move vertically, not horizontally, in this passage area?
Or maybe the blocks are constrained by the shape of the gray area. Let me look at the gray area more carefully:
- The gray area (G) forms a large rectangular chamber
- The passage (c) is a separate area that the blocks can enter
- Maybe the blocks can only move within the gray area, not in the passage
Wait, but the blocks ARE in the passage area now (rows 40-44, cols 19-23), and they got there by moving UP. So they can move into the passage.
Let me try moving UP again to see if the blocks can move further up into the vertical passage.
